era_context: Shalmaneser III's reign is in its mid-phase (858–824 BCE). The Levant is increasingly under Assyrian pressure, with regional states beginning to organize resistance. In Egypt, Osorkon II's reign continues; the 22nd Dynasty is at its height. Greece is developing city-state structures and early colonization. China's Spring and Autumn period continues with fragmentation.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's Campaigns Continue; Possible Tribute from Levantine States
events.description: Shalmaneser III's military campaigns in the Levant continue. By this period, several Levantine states are beginning to recognize Assyrian dominance and may be offering tribute or entering vassal relationships. The exact events of 862 BCE are not individually documented in surviving sources, but the year falls within the period of sustained Assyrian expansion. These campaigns establish the foundation for the major confrontation at Qarqar (853 BCE), where a coalition of western states will attempt to resist Assyrian hegemony.
events.certainty_note: The year 862 BCE is assigned by chronological convention. No specific event of this year is distinctly documented; the year is part of a broader campaign sequence.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific historical event is documented for 862 BCE. The year may be historically insignificant or simply undocumented. Modern scholarship does not treat 862 BCE as a distinct turning point.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 862 BCE is minimal. Assyrian annals do not isolate this year as significant. The broader context of Shalmaneser III's reign is well-attested, but individual years within the campaign sequence are often not separately documented unless a major event occurred. This reflects both the nature of ancient record-keeping and the loss of detailed records over time.

graph_edges.note: Part of the campaign sequence leading to the Battle of Qarqar
